metadata.from: James Edward Hanifen Sr. ('Dad') — signed 'Love Dad' on both page 2 and page 4; written on Hanifen Body & Paint Co. stationery from Des Moines, Iowa
metadata.location_written_from: Des Moines, Iowa (Hanifen Body & Paint Co., 801 Park Street)
metadata.location_sent_to: P.F.C. John R. Hanifen, 55046284, Co C. 439 Eng. Con. Bn., A.P.O. 301, San Francisco, California
metadata.envelope_notes: Sent via Air Mail. Two 3-cent Thomas Jefferson stamps. Handwritten annotation 'for Bob Hanfen' (likely indicating forwarding or noting contents for Bob). 'c/o Postmaster' noted at lower left. Initials 'JR' at bottom left corner. Return address: Hanifen Body & Paint Co., 801 Park Street, Des Moines 14, Iowa.
people_mentioned: Jim Hanifen (Jr., brother — referenced as going with Dad to send check)
metadata.mood_tone: Casual, warm, folksy; Dad is chatty and affectionate, mixing humor and concern for John, touching on family finances, weather, hunting, and worry about soldiers being taken advantage of upon return from Korea.
metadata.legibility_notes: Handwriting is flowing and informal, with many abbreviations and phonetic spellings. Some words difficult to parse due to cursive style and paper aging/staining. Page 3 in particular has some phrases that required careful interpretation.
metadata.historical_context: John is serving in the Korean War era military (Co C, 439 Eng. Con. Bn., APO 301). Dad references soldiers returning from Korea being cheated/taken advantage of financially. Letter dated November 1951, active period of Korean War.

pages.transcription:
Nov 5 – 51

Well John,
    Just a little Bull. do you know, you would be ashamed to go away with some of the Kids they take. You make good Water Boys. Ridgeway says you Boys as Stuck for 18 month. Well, it is just as Bad one Place as another they Say. We are Still getting work. But they are only 4 of us Working. down to nite trying to make Mercury Heat. don't No if I can or not. Had a Sign Put on truck the other day

HANIFEN
Body Shop
48223

[written alongside bottom text:] don't look Bad – will check my June in Bad and see if they are any Thermostats in Motors

did you get check I [and?] Jim [Jr.] sent to you, did you get large Box Hell Sent 6 or 8 Weeks ago.
If nothing Happens, will go Pheasant hunting Sunday. I guess Edith & I will go up to Her Sister Alice, to see if they are any Birds. Duck, went thru here like a Bat out of Hell, Cold hit up North, they Moved out of Minsota [Minnesota], then a Cold Blast Sent them on thru Iowa, Just a little Snow flurries here, But So early in the fall. all the Places around Iowa Caught Hell 12 inches in St. Louis today Kansas New Mexico all caught it. Boy are they having it 300 Cars got between St. Louis & K.C.
Nov 11 Bob Birth day didn't mean Say, you got Birth day Card What Happen

Love Dad

Nov 8 – 51

Dear John Jim Just got your letter & Held this up to put check in
June will go down & am Sending you another 60 [illegible word — possibly "00"] You Better Remember Jan 1 – when You Broke It. They are Sure Chomping [Cheating?] the guys as they come Back from [Korea] got a guy for $485 that He Spent in Korea

& one of his good friends took him. They all Say the Same thing didn't No you had any Money at all
Dick & I Brought Back an Elk & Deer So we have a little Meat on Hand. Still have got Work, But other Shops don't So I Guess Were lucky. Going to Edith Sister Alice Sunday & see if any Birds

Love Dad.

full_transcription:
Nov 5 – 51

Well John,
    Just a little Bull. do you know, you would be ashamed to go away with some of the Kids they take. You make good Water Boys. Ridgeway says you Boys as Stuck for 18 month. Well, it is just as Bad one Place as another they Say. We are Still getting work. But they are only 4 of us Working. down to nite trying to make Mercury Heat. don't No if I can or not. Had a Sign Put on truck the other day

HANIFEN
Body Shop
48223

[alongside:] don't look Bad – will check my June in Bad and see if they are any Thermostats in Motors

---

did you get check I [and?] Jim sent to you, did you get large Box Hell Sent 6 or 8 Weeks ago.
If nothing Happens, will go Pheasant hunting Sunday. I guess Edith & I will go up to Her Sister Alice, to see if they are any Birds. Duck, went thru here like a Bat out of Hell, Cold hit up North, they Moved out of Minsota [Minnesota], then a Cold Blast Sent them on thru Iowa, Just a little Snow flurries here, But So early in the fall. all the Places around Iowa Caught Hell 12 inches in St. Louis today Kansas New Mexico all caught it. Boy are they having it 300 Cars got between St. Louis & K.C.
Nov 11 Bob Birth day didn't mean Say, you got Birth day Card What Happen

Love Dad

---

Nov 8 – 51

Dear John Jim Just got your letter & Held this up to put check in
June will go down & am Sending you another 60 [illegible] You Better Remember Jan 1 – when You Broke It. They are Sure Chomping [Cheating?] the guys as they come Back got a guy for $485 that He Spent in Korea

---

& one of his good friends took him. They all Say the Same thing didn't No you had any Money at all
Dick & I Brought Back an Elk & Deer So we have a little Meat on Hand. Still have got Work, But other Shops don't So I Guess Were lucky. Going to Edith Sister Alice Sunday & see if any Birds

Love Dad.
summary: This is a two-part letter (begun Nov 5, completed Nov 8, 1951) from James Hanifen Sr. ('Dad') to his son John, who is serving in the military during the Korean War era. Dad updates John on the slow season at the body shop, a major early-season snowstorm hitting Iowa and surrounding states, plans for pheasant hunting with Edith (and later a deer/elk trip with Dick), and reminds John of Bob's November 11th birthday. He also mentions sending checks to John and warns him about soldiers being financially taken advantage of upon returning from Korea. The letter is warm and chatty, signed 'Love Dad' on both sections.
